will still have the lobby, but 1t will
be a real lobby. The state central
committee of the Equal Suffrage
league, of which Mrs. Helen A.
Moore is chairman, is preparing a
systematic campaign. Wives of
democrats will handle the demo.
Gratis in the state legislature and
wives of republicans will look after
the republican members. Literature
will be dispensed and modern cam
paign methods will be adopted.


Every morning the members OF
the state legislature will be asked
over the breakfast cup, What are
you going to do about it?"


The officers of the Equal Suffrage
league of California are Mrs. Mary
Sperry. president; Mrs. Lillian Har-
ris Coffin, vice president; Mrs. Ella
Mitchell, treasurer; Mrs. Nellie
Scoville, corresponding secretary.
Mrs. Theodore Pinther, ST., of
San Francisco has embroidered a
beautiful banner in colored silk
and the suffragettes will march un-
der this in the demonstrations.
Mrs. Theodore Pinther, jr., pretty
little lady who looks as though she
would fit better in picture of a
tea party than in a political con-
vention. carries the banner. She,
too. is an ardent suffragette.
